title: Error on committing worksheet on xlxs\worksheet-writer
author: king612
body:
Love this module - thanks so much for building it.

I am streaming a large amount of data (500K rows) to build a spreadsheet using the xlsx streaming framework.  I commit each row of data as I add it, and my workbook has many sheets that are created as I find certain data.  Then I commit the workbook (which I see in the code commits each worksheet), and the resulting xlsx is in fact open-able from Excel, but Excel clearly has some indigestion opening it.  It takes a long time, looks like it starts over a couple times, etc., but it does eventually open.  Also, I tried to manually commit each sheet before committing the workbook, but the streaming interface does not have the same eachSheet() features as the non-streaming one has.

I'm on a client system, so I can't post the code, but here is a summary of the (harmless?) stack trace I get on every workbook.commit():

error: uncaughtException: Cannot read property 'length' on null
worksheet-writer.js, function _nextRow, line 273
worksheet-writer.js, function addRow, line 340

I'm on 0.2.24, but I can't easily upgrade to the latest (offline system).

Any help or suggestions appreciated.

comments.author: orestis
comments.body: I was seeing a similar error, which I think is the result on trying to addRow to an already committed sheet. I was streaming from a mongo cursor using forEach, which (obviously) returns immediately — so I was committing my sheet/workbook and then adding the rows. So, in the case someone finds this error, make sure you aren't committing the sheet and then adding rows in some forgotten callback.

comments.author: mochmdruizki
comments.body: I also experienced this when I used forEach to insert rows. Then i change it to for loop because I think the problem was because we should await each iteration.
